Reasoning: The patient's current hospital visit record is dominated by severe trauma (multiple closed fractures of the acetabulum, upper end of tibia, base of skull, and multiple ribs), severe infectious processes (Methicillin susceptible pneumonia due to Staphylococcus aureus, Intestinal infection due to Clostridium difficile), and acute respiratory failure. The medication profile includes numerous agents for managing these acute conditions, such as Ipratropium Bromide Neb, Albuterol 0.083% Neb Soln, Dextrose 50%, Potassium Chloride, Duloxetine, Amitriptyline HCl, Calcium Gluconate, Psyllium Wafer, Atenolol, Lorazepam, Valsartan, Doxazosin, Simvastatin, Insulin, Vancomycin Oral Liquid, Metoprolol, HydrALAzine, Senna, Vancomycin, Iso-Osmotic Dextrose, Furosemide, Glycopyrrolate, Acetaminophen (Liquid), Propofol, Levothyroxine Sodium, Cosyntropin, Loperamide, Heparin Flush CVL (100 units/ml), NS, CefazoLIN, D5W, Midazolam, Multivitamins, Fentanyl Citrate,
